title: Ascochilus emarginatus
taxonomy: Ascochilus emarginatus belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within this family, Ascochilus emarginatus is classified under the subfamily Aeridinae, a subfamily known for its delicate and often fragrant flowers. The genus Ascochilus is a small group of orchids that are primarily found in Southeast Asia and are characterized by their unique flowers and growth habits. Ascochilus emarginatus is a species within this genus, identified by its distinctive emarginate leaves and other specific characteristics. The classification of Ascochilus emarginatus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species and provides a framework for understanding its biology and ecology.
